steps.title: Modify the subject
steps.content: The main subject is "Antiquarians," and the main verb is "might procure." The blank must describe which antiquarians are being discussed without creating a second main verb before "might procure."

steps.title: Reject second main verbs
steps.content: A, B, and D are finite verb forms. They would give "Antiquarians" a separate main verb before "might procure," creating an uncoordinated structure. A participle keeps "hoping to read" as a modifier of "Antiquarians." The answer is C.
